prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French: "Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'importe quel argument aurait pu le faire."
Japanese: "我々の間の沈黙は、どんな論争よりも多くを語っていた。(Wareware no aida no chinmoku wa, donna ronsou yori mo ouku o katararete ita.)"
Arabic: "الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما كان يمكن لأي جدال أن يقوله. (As-samt bayna-na qaa-la akthar mimma kaana yumkin li-ay jidal an yaqoolah.)"

Note: The phrase 'said more than any argument could have' is a bit tricky as it involves a hypothetical comparison. In all the translations, the structure is modified slightly to maintain the intended meaning.
